violations.text: The presence of pests is not controlled as evidenced by many crows observed in and around the dumpsters. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Establishment cleaned all trash from around the dumpsters to remove attractants and harborage conditions. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2026

The refuse area observed with a large amount of trash and boxes overflowing the dumpsters and surrounding the dumpsters. Establishment had employees clean trash from around the dumpsters so that Waste Management could empty the dumpster. New dumpsters were delivered to the site during inspection. The new dumpsters were then filled with surrounding trash for pick-up tomorrow. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2026

A large amount of litter and trash observed in the dumpster area. Establishments and its premises shall be kept free of litter and trash to prevent harborage conditions. Establishment cleaned up dumpster area during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2026

Some deli meat and cheese observed not marked with the date of preparation/slicing. All ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared/sliced in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation/slicing. New Violation. Correct By: 12/30/2025
Spray bottles used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/30/2025

The doors to the outside, located at the entrance, are not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 12/30/2025
The door to the restroom is not self-closing. New Violation. Correct By: 12/30/2025

The dumpster outside was observed with an excess of litter, trash, and cardboard. Refuse areas must be maintained. Corrective Action - Establishment is working to clean area of refuse. Establishment is calling Dumpster company for an additional pick up today. Establishment is to work to get daily dumpster service. New Violation. Correct By: 12/30/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (spatula, slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/03/2025

The doors to the outside, located at the entrance, are not tight-fitting. Space observed at the bottom between the doors. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/27/2025
Employees were observed putting gloves on/changing gloves without washing their hands. Food employees must wash their hands before putting on gloves. New Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2025
Food debris observed in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. New Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (slicer, knives) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2025

Deli meats observed not marked with the date of opening/preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods opened/sliced/prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2025

Working container of dry ingredient observed not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2025

The doors to the outside, located at the entrance, are not tight-fitting. Space observed between the doors at the bottom. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 01/27/2025
Food debris observed in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/09/2024

The doors to the outside, located at the store entrance (between the 2 doors), are not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/09/2024

The doors to the outside, located at the entrance, are not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 11/15/2023

The sinks used for food preparation and the 3-bay sink are lacking proper air gaps. A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. New Violation. Correct By: 11/15/2023
